name: code-review
description: Run a multi-perspective review on a PR or recent diff, then post the verdict.

steps.prompt: Identify the changed files in the current branch relative to the default branch. Use `git diff --name-only origin/main...HEAD` and produce a per-file summary of the changes. Save the summary under .guild/runtime/code-review/diff.md.

Run a Cleric-style code-quality review on the diff. Focus on correctness, naming, error handling, and test coverage. Use the review_models entry if configured. Write the verdict under .guild/runtime/code-review/quality.md.
Run a Paladin-style security review on the diff. Focus on auth, crypto, input validation, secrets, and OWASP Top 10. Write the verdict under .guild/runtime/code-review/security.md.

Combine the quality and security verdicts into a single review. Group findings by severity. Mark each finding with the file, line range, and a short remediation. If both reviews are APPROVE, the synthesis is APPROVE. Otherwise it is REQUEST_CHANGES.
Show the synthesis. The user will edit it inline or approve before posting.
Post the synthesis as a PR review comment using the gh CLI. If the PR is not yet open, report the verdict to the chat instead.
